Summary: Smart Expense Categorization — ML model integration
Description: Integrate the ML categorization model into the expense submission flow. When a user submits a receipt, the system should auto-suggest a category with a confidence score. Users can accept or override. Override data feeds back into model training. Depends on SPEND-148 for receipt OCR improvements.

Improve OCR extraction for merchant name, amount, currency, and date. Current accuracy is 74% — target is 90%+ before we can ship auto-categorization. Focus on multi-currency receipts and handwritten amounts.
Define and implement confidence thresholds: above 85% = auto-apply, 60-85% = suggest with confirmation, below 60% = manual entry. Needs UX review with Marina.
Complete redesign of the group booking experience. New flow: organizer creates trip → invites travelers → each traveler selects preferences → system finds optimal bundle. Shipped to 100% on March 18.
Build internal dashboard tracking: group booking conversion rate, avg group size, time to complete booking, traveler satisfaction. Pull from Amplitude + backend events.

Summary: Group Booking — edge case: mixed cabin class preferences
Description: When travelers in a group select different cabin classes, the bundling logic breaks. Need to handle mixed-class groups gracefully. Low volume but causes support tickets.

Summary: Policy Engine v2 — configurable spend rules
Description: Allow admins to create custom spend rules: per-trip caps, category restrictions, approval chains by amount threshold. BLOCKED: waiting on Payments API v3 endpoint for real-time balance checks. ETA from Payments team: April 4.

Summary: Policy Engine v2 — rule builder UI
Description: Design and build the rule configuration UI. Admin selects conditions (amount, category, traveler group) and actions (auto-approve, require approval, block). Marina has Figma mocks ready — eng build started.

Summary: Policy Engine v2 — approval chain notifications
Description: When a spend rule triggers an approval requirement, notify the right approver via email and Slack. Include expense details, policy rule that triggered, and one-click approve/reject.

Summary: Expense report PDF export — formatting fixes
Description: Currency symbols were rendering incorrectly in PDF exports for GBP and EUR. Fixed. Also improved table alignment for multi-line item descriptions.

Summary: Slack bot — weekly spend summary for managers
Description: Bot posts a weekly summary to #finance-leads every Monday: total spend by category, biggest line items, policy violations flagged. Tudor working on the formatting and opt-in preferences.

Summary: Mobile app — receipt capture camera improvements
Description: Camera overlay guidelines for receipt capture. Auto-crop and perspective correction. Feeds into SPEND-148 OCR pipeline.
